wiki_catalogs.name: Project Overview
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive content for the MatricMaster AI project overview section. Explain the platform's purpose as an educational tool for South African Grade 12 students, its core mission of helping students master matric exams through interactive practice, and its unique value proposition combining past papers, AI-powered explanations, and gamified learning. Document the target audience, key features including interactive past papers, AI-powered explanations, progress tracking, personalized study plans, and gamification elements. Describe the technology stack including Next.js 16, React 19, Tailwind CSS 4, Radix UI, Google Gemini API, and TypeScript. Include both conceptual overviews for stakeholders and technical details for developers. Use terminology consistent with the codebase and provide practical examples demonstrating the platform's educational impact.

wiki_catalogs.name: Schema Design
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive data model documentation for MatricMaster AI's database schema design. Detail all entity relationships including users, sessions, accounts, subjects, questions, options, and search history tables. Document field definitions, data types, constraints, and indexes for each table. Explain the Drizzle ORM integration patterns, primary/foreign key relationships, and referential integrity constraints. Include database schema diagrams showing entity relationships and cardinality. Document the Better Auth schema integration and how it extends the core application tables. Specify data validation rules, business constraints, and data lifecycle management. Address indexing strategies for query optimization and performance considerations.

wiki_catalogs.name: Authentication Architecture
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive authentication architecture documentation for MatricMaster AI. Detail the Better Auth integration pattern including the factory pattern implementation, lazy initialization, and proxy-based access. Explain the authentication instance creation process, database adapter configuration, and environment variable validation. Document the authentication lifecycle from initialization to runtime access, including connection pooling and error handling strategies. Cover the modular architecture design that separates authentication concerns from database management. Include implementation details for the authentication router setup, base URL configuration, and trusted origins management. Provide code examples showing proper initialization patterns and access methods. Address the proxy pattern usage for seamless authentication instance access and explain the benefits of this architectural approach.

wiki_catalogs.name: Primitive Components
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for MatricMaster AI's primitive UI components built on Radix UI foundations. Document the button component with its variant system (default, destructive, outline, secondary, ghost, link, ios), size variations (default, sm, lg, icon), and slot composition pattern. Cover input components with validation states, placeholder handling, and accessibility features. Detail card components for content organization, avatar components for user representation, badge components for status indicators, and separator components for visual grouping. Include prop specifications, usage examples, styling customization options, and integration patterns with other components. Address component composition, state management, and responsive behavior.

wiki_catalogs.name: Past Paper Architecture
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for the past paper system architecture. Explain the component structure including PastPapers screen for listing and filtering, PastPaperViewer for individual paper display, and mock data management. Document the data flow from mock data sources to UI components, state management patterns for search and filtering, and routing mechanisms between paper listings and individual viewers. Detail the responsive design implementation, scroll area handling, and mobile optimization strategies. Include component composition patterns, prop drilling considerations, and performance optimizations for large datasets. Address the integration with Next.js App Router and client-side navigation patterns.

wiki_catalogs.name: Quiz Architecture
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for the MatricMaster AI quiz architecture. Detail the core quiz engine implementation including state management, component lifecycle, and user interaction patterns. Document the quiz flow control mechanism, answer selection logic, and state transitions between different quiz phases. Explain the integration with AI explanation services and the conditional rendering system for feedback cards. Include implementation details for progress tracking, scoring mechanisms, and navigation between questions. Document the component composition patterns, prop drilling strategies, and event handling architecture. Provide examples of quiz state management, animation triggers, and responsive design considerations. Address performance optimization techniques for smooth user interactions and memory management for large quiz datasets.

wiki_catalogs.name: Study Path Creation
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed content for the study path creation and management system. Document the step-by-step wizard interface for subject selection, weekly commitment configuration, and AI-generated study plans. Explain the subject selection grid with icons and color coding, slider-based weekly hour configuration, and the AI-powered study plan generation process. Detail the study path visualization showing completed, current, and locked milestones. Include implementation specifics for subject mapping, study plan algorithm integration, and user preference handling. Provide examples of different study path configurations, subject combinations, and weekly commitment scenarios. Address the integration with Gemini AI for personalized study recommendations and the fallback mechanisms when AI generation fails.

wiki_catalogs.name: AI Service Architecture
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for the AI service architecture in MatricMaster AI. Detail the Google Gemini API wrapper implementation including service initialization, configuration management, and API endpoint abstraction. Document the server action pattern implementation for secure AI interactions, explaining how server actions encapsulate AI API calls and handle authentication. Explain the modular service design with separate functions for different AI capabilities (explanation generation, study planning, smart search). Include implementation details for API key management, environment variable configuration, and error handling strategies. Document the utility functions used for input validation, response processing, and data transformation. Provide examples of service instantiation, method invocation patterns, and integration with the Next.js App Router architecture.

wiki_catalogs.name: UploadThing Integration
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for UploadThing integration in MatricMaster AI. Explain the UploadThing configuration setup, router definition, and React helper generation. Document the file routing system, endpoint registration, and API route structure. Detail the integration between client-side upload helpers and server-side handlers. Include examples of file router configuration, endpoint customization, and error handling patterns. Explain the relationship between UploadThing and the broader application architecture, including authentication integration and permission management.

wiki_catalogs.name: Profile Management
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed content for the user profile management system. Document the profile page implementation including avatar display, user information presentation, and verification badges. Explain the statistics visualization using radar charts for academic performance comparison against provincial averages. Detail the achievement system integration showing skill badges and progress recognition. Cover the tabbed interface for switching between personal stats and comparative analysis views. Document the chart configuration, data structures for performance metrics, and achievement data models. Include implementation details for image handling, gradient effects, and responsive design patterns. Provide examples of profile customization options, data validation, and user preference management within the profile context.

wiki_catalogs.name: Authentication APIs
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive API documentation for MatricMaster AI's authentication endpoints. Document the Better Auth integration endpoints under /api/auth/[...better-auth] including GET and POST methods for login, logout, registration, and session management. Detail the authentication flow, session handling, and token management. Include request/response schemas for login credentials, user registration, password reset, and social authentication. Document authentication middleware, session validation, and security headers. Provide examples of client-side authentication implementation, cookie handling, and error responses. Address rate limiting, account lockout policies, and security best practices for authentication endpoints.

wiki_catalogs.name: Mathematics Model
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive data modeling documentation for the Mathematics subject model used in MatricMaster AI. Detail the recursive question structure with hierarchical IDs, marks allocation, and nested question formats. Document the ExamPaper interface with metadata including exam type, year, session, and total marks. Explain the DbQuestionItem structure for database normalization with parent-child relationships and depth indexing. Include implementation examples for question extraction, LaTeX rendering integration, and mobile optimization strategies. Document the curriculum alignment with South African Mathematics standards and provide guidance for content validation and quality assurance processes.

wiki_catalogs.name: Getting Started
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive getting started documentation for MatricMaster AI. Include detailed prerequisites covering Node.js 20+, npm/yarn/bun package managers, and Google Gemini API key setup. Provide step-by-step installation instructions including repository cloning, dependency installation, environment variable configuration, and development server startup. Document the complete development workflow from initial setup to running the first test. Include troubleshooting common setup issues, environment configuration best practices, and verification steps to ensure proper installation. Address both local development and production deployment scenarios. Make the content accessible to developers with varying experience levels while ensuring all technical requirements are clearly specified.

wiki_catalogs.name: ORM Integration
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for Drizzle ORM integration in MatricMaster AI. Explain the ORM configuration setup, database connection management, and type-safe query building patterns. Document the database client initialization, connection pooling strategies, and transaction handling. Detail the schema mapping between TypeScript interfaces and database tables. Include examples of CRUD operations, complex queries, and relation fetching patterns. Explain the Better Auth integration with custom database adapters and how it leverages the ORM for session management. Document type inference patterns, query builder usage, and performance optimization techniques.

wiki_catalogs.name: Authentication Providers
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed authentication providers documentation for MatricMaster AI. Document the supported authentication methods including Google OAuth, Twitter OAuth, and email/password authentication. Explain the provider configuration process, client ID/secret management, and environment variable setup. Detail the conditional provider loading mechanism that enables/disables Twitter authentication based on credential availability. Include implementation examples for each provider type, form handling, and user registration flows. Document the social login integration patterns, callback handling, and user account linking strategies. Provide troubleshooting guides for common provider configuration issues, OAuth flow problems, and credential validation errors. Address security considerations for each provider type and explain the anonymous session plugin integration.

wiki_catalogs.name: Form Components
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed documentation for MatricMaster AI's form component suite designed for educational platform interactions. Document input components with validation states, error handling, and autocomplete functionality. Cover label components for form accessibility and user guidance. Detail select components with option filtering, multi-select capabilities, and custom rendering. Document textarea components for long-form content, checkbox components for binary choices, radio groups for mutually exclusive selections, switch components for on/off toggles, and slider components for numeric ranges. Include form validation patterns, accessibility compliance (ARIA labels, keyboard navigation), error state management, and integration with form libraries. Provide examples of complex form compositions and validation workflows.

wiki_catalogs.name: Content Management
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed content for past paper content management. Document the mock data structure used for demonstration purposes, including paper metadata fields (subject, year, month, marks, time, downloadUrl). Explain the content organization by subject and year, data validation rules, and mock data generation patterns. Detail how real content would be integrated with UploadThing for actual file management, including content CRUD operations and database schema considerations. Include examples of adding new subjects, updating paper metadata, and managing content lifecycle. Address content discovery mechanisms, categorization strategies, and integration with the broader educational content ecosystem.

wiki_catalogs.name: Question Management
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed documentation for the question management system in MatricMaster AI. Explain the question data structure, including question text, answer options, correct answers, and metadata fields. Document the mock data organization, question categorization by subject and difficulty level, and content validation processes. Detail the question sourcing mechanisms, data loading strategies, and caching approaches. Include implementation examples for question retrieval, randomization algorithms, and filtering by topic or difficulty. Document the relationship between question data and UI rendering components, including dynamic content generation and accessibility considerations. Address question content management workflows, bulk data operations, and integration with external content sources. Provide guidelines for extending the question database and adding new question types.

wiki_catalogs.name: Progress Tracking and Analytics
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for the progress tracking and analytics system within the study planning framework. Document the study path visualization showing completion status, streak maintenance, and milestone achievements. Explain progress calculation methods, completion percentage tracking, and study hour logging. Detail the integration between quiz performance and study path progression, allowing adaptive learning recommendations based on user performance. Include implementation details for progress data collection, analytics calculations, and visualization components. Provide examples of progress metrics, completion tracking, and performance analytics. Address the correlation between quiz scores, study hours, and overall academic improvement tracking.

wiki_catalogs.name: AI-Powered Features
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed documentation for MatricMaster AI's AI-powered features and capabilities. Document the concept explanation system that provides contextual help during quiz interactions, including subject-specific explanations and topic-based learning support. Explain the study plan generation feature that creates personalized weekly study schedules based on student preferences and available time. Detail the smart search functionality that enhances content discovery and learning resource recommendations. Document the interactive quiz assistance system that provides hints, explanations, and adaptive difficulty adjustment. Include implementation examples showing how these features integrate with the frontend components, handle user interactions, and provide value-added educational experiences. Address the prompt engineering strategies, response formatting, and content personalization techniques used in each feature.

wiki_catalogs.name: File Validation and Security
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop comprehensive documentation for file validation and security measures in the upload system. Detail the file type validation rules, size restrictions, and content scanning mechanisms. Document the security policies including file sanitization, malicious content detection, and access control enforcement. Explain the validation pipeline from client upload to server processing, including MIME type checking and file extension validation. Include examples of custom validation rules, security headers configuration, and protection against common file upload vulnerabilities. Address file naming strategies, temporary storage handling, and cleanup procedures.

wiki_catalogs.name: Dashboard and Analytics
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for the user dashboard and analytics system. Detail the dashboard layout showing user progress indicators, recent activity feeds, and personalized content recommendations. Document the analytics integration displaying academic performance trends, subject-wise progress tracking, and comparative analysis features. Explain the data visualization components including radar charts for performance comparison, achievement displays, and statistical summaries. Cover the dashboard widgets for overall averages, top subjects, and progress milestones. Include implementation details for data fetching, chart rendering, and real-time updates. Document the integration with user authentication context, session management, and personalized content delivery based on user progress and preferences.

wiki_catalogs.name: Database and System APIs
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed API documentation for MatricMaster AI's database and system management endpoints. Document the database initialization endpoint (/api/db/init) including schema creation, migration execution, and seed data setup. Detail the health check endpoint (/api/health) with system status monitoring, database connectivity verification, and service availability indicators. Include request/response formats, error handling, and diagnostic information returned by health checks. Document database connection management, transaction handling, and cleanup procedures. Provide examples of database initialization scripts, health monitoring implementations, and system maintenance workflows. Address environment-specific configurations and operational procedures.

wiki_catalogs.name: Physics Model

wiki_catalogs.name: Architecture Overview
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create architectural documentation for MatricMaster AI's system design. Describe the high-level architecture including the Next.js 16 App Router structure, component hierarchy, and data flow patterns. Document the modular monolith approach with clear separation of concerns between frontend components, backend API routes, database layer, and AI integration services. Explain the authentication architecture using Better Auth, database design with Drizzle ORM, and AI service integration patterns. Include system boundaries, component interactions, and integration patterns between different layers. Address technical decisions around server actions, client-server communication, and state management. Document infrastructure requirements, scalability considerations, and deployment topology. Provide system context diagrams showing how different components interact and explain cross-cutting concerns like security, monitoring, and performance optimization.

wiki_catalogs.name: Migration Management
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for database migration management in MatricMaster AI. Detail the migration workflow using Drizzle Kit, including migration creation, execution, and rollback procedures. Document the migration file structure, version control integration, and deployment strategies. Explain the journal system for tracking applied migrations and conflict resolution. Include examples of schema changes, data migrations, and backward compatibility maintenance. Document best practices for production deployments, testing migration scripts, and handling failed migrations. Address migration automation, CI/CD integration, and database state synchronization across environments.

wiki_catalogs.name: Session Management
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed session management documentation for MatricMaster AI. Explain the Better Auth session configuration including expiration settings, update intervals, and session persistence strategies. Document the session lifecycle from creation to destruction, including automatic renewal and cleanup processes. Detail the client-side session handling using the auth-client utility, including session validation, user context management, and protected route access. Explain the session storage mechanisms, cookie configuration, and cross-origin handling. Include implementation examples for session checking, user authentication state management, and session-based authorization patterns. Document the session security features including CSRF protection, secure cookies, and session hijacking prevention. Address common session-related issues such as expired sessions, concurrent logins, and session invalidation scenarios.

wiki_catalogs.name: Layout Components
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for MatricMaster AI's layout and container components that organize educational content. Detail sheet components for slide-out interfaces and settings panels. Document tab components for content organization and navigation. Include carousel components for content carousels and image galleries. Cover scroll area components for custom scrolling experiences and infinite lists. Detail chart components for data visualization and progress tracking. wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for PDF viewing and download functionality. Provide implementation guidelines for customizing scoring rules and integrating with external assessment systems.

Document the integration with Google Gemini API, request/response formatting, and error handling strategies. Detail the smart hint system implementation, concept clarification algorithms, and personalized learning path recommendations. Include examples of AI explanation prompts, response processing, and user interface integration for AI-powered feedback. Document the rate limiting mechanisms, caching strategies for AI responses, and offline fallback options. Address the AI explanation workflow, from question context extraction to explanation delivery, and the integration with user progress tracking. Provide implementation guidelines for extending AI capabilities, customizing explanation styles, and integrating alternative AI models for enhanced quiz functionality.

wiki_catalogs.name: Performance Optimization
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for performance optimization strategies in the AI integration system. Document the caching mechanisms implemented to reduce API calls and improve response times for frequently requested explanations and study plans. Explain the request batching and queuing strategies used to manage concurrent AI operations efficiently. Detail the cost optimization techniques including token usage management, response size optimization, and intelligent retry mechanisms. Document the monitoring and analytics implementation for tracking AI API performance, usage patterns, and cost metrics. Include implementation examples of performance tuning, memory management for large responses, and optimization techniques for different AI operation types. Address scalability considerations, load balancing strategies, and capacity planning for growing AI usage demands.

wiki_catalogs.name: API Security and Error Handling
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for MatricMaster AI's API security measures and error handling mechanisms. Document authentication middleware, request validation, and authorization policies across all API endpoints. Detail error response formats, status codes, and error classification systems. Include rate limiting implementation, IP blocking, and abuse prevention measures. Document CORS configuration, content security policies, and input sanitization. Provide examples of proper error handling in client applications, retry mechanisms, and graceful degradation strategies. Address security best practices, vulnerability mitigation, and incident response procedures. Include monitoring and logging strategies for API usage and security events.

wiki_catalogs.name: History Model
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive data modeling documentation for the History subject model used in MatricMaster AI. Detail the question structure for historical analysis, timeline construction, and source evaluation. Document the metadata handling for historical periods, geographical contexts, and cultural movements. Explain the integration with historical artifact analysis, primary source interpretation, and comparative historical studies. Include examples of ancient civilizations, colonial periods, and modern era question formats. Document the database schema for handling historical chronologies, map integration, and multimedia historical content. Provide implementation guidance for historical accuracy validation, bias detection, and curriculum alignment with South African history standards.

wiki_catalogs.name: UI Component System
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for MatricMaster AI's UI component system. Describe the component architecture built on Radix UI primitives with custom enhancements for the educational platform. Document the theme system implementation, dark mode support, and responsive design patterns. Include comprehensive component documentation for all reusable UI elements including buttons, cards, dialogs, inputs, and specialized components like charts and progress indicators. Provide usage examples with code snippets, prop specifications, and customization options. Document component composition patterns, accessibility compliance, and cross-browser compatibility. Address styling strategies using Tailwind CSS, component state management, and animation patterns. Include guidelines for extending the component library and maintaining design consistency across the platform.

wiki_catalogs.name: Accounting Model
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed data modeling documentation for the Accounting subject model in MatricMaster AI. Document the question structure for financial statements, accounting principles, and business calculations. Detail the metadata requirements for accounting cycles, journal entries, and financial reporting. Explain the integration with spreadsheet analysis, ledger management, and tax calculation components. Include examples of balance sheets, income statements, and cash flow analysis questions. Document the database schema for handling financial data, ratio calculations, and audit trail requirements. Provide implementation guidance for accounting content validation, regulatory compliance integration, and professional accounting standards alignment.

wiki_catalogs.name: Quiz System
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for MatricMaster AI's quiz system architecture. Detail the quiz engine implementation including question management, answer processing, scoring algorithms, and progress tracking. Document the different quiz types including subject-specific quizzes (Mathematics, Physics), practice quizzes, and interactive quizzes. Explain the question data structure, answer validation logic, and evaluation mechanisms. Include implementation details for quiz flow management, timing controls, and adaptive difficulty adjustment. Document the integration with AI explanations for incorrect answers and the relationship with user progress tracking. Provide examples of quiz configuration, question sourcing from mock data, and performance optimization techniques. Address quiz analytics, user engagement metrics, and content management workflows.

wiki_catalogs.name: Past Paper System
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for MatricMaster AI's past paper system. Document the past paper management architecture including PDF viewing capabilities, download handling, and content organization. Explain the integration with UploadThing for file management and the mock data structure used for demonstration purposes. Detail the past paper filtering and search functionality, subject categorization, and content discovery mechanisms. Include implementation details for PDF rendering, pagination handling, and mobile optimization. Document the relationship between past papers and quiz system, allowing students to practice with real NSC exam questions. Provide examples of past paper configuration, content management workflows, and performance optimization for large PDF files. Address accessibility considerations for PDF viewing and integration with study planning features.

wiki_catalogs.name: AI Integration
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for MatricMaster AI's AI integration system powered by Google Gemini API. Detail the AI service architecture including API wrapper implementation, input validation, response processing, and error handling strategies. Document the various AI-powered features including concept explanations, study plan generation, smart search capabilities, and interactive quiz assistance. Explain the server action patterns for secure AI interactions, rate limiting implementation, and cost optimization strategies. Include implementation details for AI prompt engineering, response formatting, and integration with the quiz system for providing contextual help. Document the AI action handlers, input sanitization, and safety measures for educational content. Provide examples of AI integration patterns, performance optimization techniques, and troubleshooting guides for API connectivity issues.

wiki_catalogs.name: Study Planning System
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for MatricMaster AI's study planning and progress tracking system. Document the study path creation workflow, weekly planning capabilities, and progress visualization features. Explain the achievement system implementation including criteria definition, reward mechanisms, and streak maintenance. Detail the leaderboard functionality for student competition and motivation. Include implementation details for study path generation algorithms, progress calculation methods, and milestone tracking. Document the integration between study planning and quiz system, allowing personalized learning recommendations. Provide examples of study path configuration, achievement criteria setup, and progress analytics. Address user preferences handling, adaptive study recommendations, and gamification mechanics that enhance student engagement and learning outcomes.

wiki_catalogs.name: User Management
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for MatricMaster AI's user management system. Detail the profile management functionality including user data handling, preference settings, and account customization options. Document the dashboard implementation showing user progress, recent activity, and personalized content recommendations. Explain the user context management, session handling, and data privacy measures. Include implementation details for user data validation, profile updates, and preference persistence. Document the integration between user management and other system features including progress tracking, achievement systems, and study planning. Provide examples of user data structures, profile customization workflows, and user analytics collection. Address user onboarding flows, data protection compliance, and user experience optimization for different user types and learning preferences.

wiki_catalogs.name: File Upload System
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for MatricMaster AI's file upload system powered by UploadThing. Detail the file upload architecture including validation rules, storage management, and access control mechanisms. Document the supported file types, size limitations, and security measures for file handling. Explain the integration with past paper system for PDF uploads and the content management workflows. Include implementation details for file validation, thumbnail generation, and CDN integration. Document the upload progress tracking, error handling, and retry mechanisms. Provide examples of file upload configuration, security best practices, and performance optimization for large file handling. Address file access permissions, content moderation, and integration with the broader content management system.

wiki_catalogs.name: API Reference
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive API documentation for MatricMaster AI's backend endpoints. Document all RESTful API endpoints including authentication endpoints (/api/auth/...), database initialization (/api/db/init), health check (/api/health), Open Graph generation (/api/og), and file upload APIs (/api/uploadthing). For each endpoint, specify HTTP methods, URL patterns, request/response schemas, and authentication requirements. Include detailed parameter descriptions, expected response formats, and error codes. Document the authentication flow for protected endpoints, rate limiting policies, and security considerations. Provide practical examples of API usage, client implementation guidelines, and integration patterns. Address CORS configuration, content-type handling, and API versioning strategies. Include troubleshooting guides for common API issues and performance optimization tips for high-traffic scenarios.

wiki_catalogs.name: Testing Strategy
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive testing strategy documentation for MatricMaster AI. Detail the end-to-end testing approach using Playwright including test configuration, page object patterns, and cross-browser testing capabilities. Document the test suite structure covering authentication flows, navigation testing, and core feature validation. Explain the testing workflow including test execution, reporting, and CI/CD integration. Include implementation details for test data management, mocking strategies, and test environment setup. Document the test results analysis, screenshot capture, and trace file interpretation for debugging failed tests. Provide examples of writing effective tests, test maintenance strategies, and performance testing approaches. Address test coverage analysis, accessibility testing, and continuous integration pipelines for automated testing.

wiki_catalogs.name: Development Guidelines
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive development guidelines for MatricMaster AI project contributors. Detail the code standards including TypeScript configuration, Biome linting rules, and formatting conventions. Document the component development patterns, architectural guidelines, and best practices for extending the platform. Explain the database migration procedures, schema modification workflows, and data seeding strategies. Include implementation details for environment configuration, build processes, and deployment procedures. Document the modernization efforts, code refactoring guidelines, and technical debt management. Provide examples of proper component implementation, service integration patterns, and testing methodologies. Address code review processes, contribution workflows, and quality assurance measures. Include troubleshooting guides for common development issues and optimization strategies for development efficiency.

wiki_catalogs.name: Data Modeling
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive data modeling documentation for MatricMaster AI's educational content structure. Detail the subject-specific data models for Mathematics, Physics, Economics, English, History, and Accounting aligned with South African curriculum standards. Document the question structures, answer formats, difficulty levels, and topic coverage for each subject. Explain the content organization patterns, prerequisite relationships, and learning pathway mapping. Include implementation details for content validation, question randomization, and adaptive assessment strategies. Document the relationship between data models and quiz system functionality, progress tracking, and study planning features. Provide examples of content creation workflows, data import procedures, and curriculum alignment validation. Address content maintenance strategies, update procedures, and integration with AI-powered content generation.

wiki_catalogs.name: Deployment and Operations
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive deployment and operations documentation for MatricMaster AI. Detail the production deployment process including build optimization, environment configuration, and hosting platform setup. Document the CI/CD pipeline configuration using GitHub Actions, automated testing integration, and deployment automation. Explain the environment variable management, database configuration, and external service integration for production environments. Include implementation details for performance optimization, monitoring setup, and logging strategies. Document the deployment topology, scaling considerations, and disaster recovery procedures. Provide examples of production troubleshooting, performance monitoring, and maintenance procedures. Address security hardening, SSL/TLS configuration, and operational best practices for educational platform hosting. Include rollback procedures, backup strategies, and incident response protocols.

wiki_overview.content:
<blog>

# MatricMaster AI: Comprehensive Educational Platform Analysis

## Project Introduction

### Purpose Statement
MatricMaster AI is an innovative educational platform designed specifically for South African Grade 12 students to master their matriculation exams through interactive learning experiences. The platform combines traditional study materials with cutting-edge AI-powered assistance to create a comprehensive learning ecosystem.

### Core Goals and Objectives
- **Exam Preparation**: Provide comprehensive preparation resources for South African National Senior Certificate (NSC) examinations
- **Interactive Learning**: Deliver engaging, hands-on practice through interactive quizzes and past paper access
- **AI Integration**: Leverage Google Gemini AI for personalized explanations and study guidance
- **Accessibility**: Ensure the platform is accessible to students across South Africa's diverse educational landscape
- **Performance Tracking**: Enable students to monitor their progress and identify knowledge gaps

### Target Audience
- South African Grade 12 students preparing for NSC examinations
- Students seeking supplementary educational support beyond traditional classroom learning
- Learners requiring personalized study guidance and exam preparation strategies

## Technical Architecture

### Component Breakdown

The application follows a modern Next.js 16 architecture with a clear separation of concerns:

```mermaid
graph TD
    A[Root Layout] --> B[Theme Provider]
    A --> C[Error Boundary]
    A --> D[Mobile Frame]
    
    B --> E[UI Components]
    C --> F[Authentication Layer]
    D --> G[Screens/Pages]
    
    E --> H[Radix UI Primitives]
    E --> I[Custom Components]
    
    F --> J[Better Auth]
    F --> K[Session Management]
    
    G --> L[Landing Page]
    G --> M[Dashboard]
    G --> N[Quiz System]
    G --> O[Past Papers]
    G --> P[Profile Management]
```

### Design Patterns

The project implements several key architectural patterns:

1. **Repository Pattern**: Database operations are abstracted through dedicated managers
2. **Factory Pattern**: Authentication instances are created dynamically based on configuration
3. **Proxy Pattern**: Authentication methods are accessed through a proxy for lazy initialization
4. **Component Composition**: UI components follow Radix UI's composition patterns
5. **Server Actions**: AI functionality is encapsulated in server-side actions for security

### System Relationships

```mermaid
flowchart TD
    A[Client Browser] --> B[Next.js App Router]
    B --> C[Server Actions]
    B --> D[Static Assets]
    
    C --> E[Google Gemini API]
    C --> F[UploadThing Storage]
    
    B --> G[Database Layer]
    G --> H[PostgreSQL]
    
    B --> I[Authentication]
    I --> J[Better Auth]
    J --> K[Session Store]
```

### Data Flow Diagrams

```mermaid
sequenceDiagram
    participant U as User
    participant A as AI Service
    participant G as Gemini API
    participant DB as Database
    
    U->>A: Request explanation
    A->>G: Generate content
    G-->>A: AI Response
    A-->>U: Formatted explanation
    
    U->>A: Study plan request
    A->>G: Generate study plan
    G-->>A: Structured plan
    A->>DB: Store preferences
    DB-->>A: Confirmation
    A-->>U: Personalized plan
```

## Key Implementation

### Main Entry Points
- **Application Root**: `src/app/layout.tsx` - Central layout with metadata and theme provider
- **Homepage**: `src/app/page.tsx` - Landing page featuring subject exploration
- **API Routes**: `src/app/api/` - Server endpoints for authentication, database initialization, and health checks

### Core Modules

#### Authentication System
- **Auth Configuration**: `src/lib/auth.ts` - Better Auth integration with dynamic database adapter
- **Database Manager**: `src/lib/db/index.ts` - Singleton pattern for PostgreSQL connections
- **Session Handling**: Automatic session persistence with configurable expiration

#### AI Integration
- **Gemini Service**: `src/services/geminiService.ts` - High-level AI service wrapper
- **AI Actions**: `src/services/aiActions.ts` - Server actions for secure AI interactions
- **Input Validation**: Zod schemas for all AI input parameters

#### UI Component System
- **Component Library**: `src/components/ui/` - Radix UI-based primitives with custom styling
- **Custom Components**: `src/components/` - Application-specific components like avatars and cards
- **Theme Provider**: `src/components/theme-provider.tsx` - Dark/light mode support

### Configuration Approach

#### Environment Configuration
- **Database**: `.env.local` - PostgreSQL connection strings and credentials
- **Authentication**: Social login credentials for Google and optional Twitter
- **AI Services**: `GEMINI_API_KEY` for AI-powered features
- **Application**: `NEXT_PUBLIC_APP_URL` for base URLs and CORS configuration

#### Build Configuration
- **Next.js Config**: `next.config.mjs` - Image optimization and experimental features
- **Tailwind CSS**: `components.json` - Component library configuration
- **TypeScript**: Strict typing with comprehensive type definitions

### External Dependencies

#### Core Framework Dependencies
- **Next.js 16**: React-based web framework with App Router
- **Better Auth**: Authentication solution with database adapters
- **Drizzle ORM**: PostgreSQL ORM with TypeScript support
- **Radix UI**: Unstyled, accessible component library

#### AI and Media Dependencies
- **Google Gemini**: AI model integration for explanations and study planning
- **UploadThing**: File upload and storage solutions
- **Lucide React**: Icon library for consistent UI elements

#### Development Tools
- **Biome**: Code quality and formatting tool
- **Playwright**: End-to-end testing framework
- **Drizzle Kit**: Database migration and schema management

### Integration Points

#### Database Schema
```mermaid
erDiagram
    USER ||--o{ SESSION : authenticates
    USER ||--o{ PAST_PAPER : accesses
    USER ||--o{ QUIZ_ATTEMPT : completes
    USER ||--o{ STUDY_PLAN : follows
    
    SESSION ||--|| USER : belongs_to
    PAST_PAPER ||--|| SUBJECT : covers
    QUIZ_ATTEMPT ||--|| QUESTION : answers
    STUDY_PLAN ||--|| SUBJECT : targets
```

#### API Endpoints
- **Authentication**: `/api/auth/[...better-auth]` - OAuth and session management
- **Database**: `/api/db/init` - Database initialization and health checks
- **Storage**: `/api/uploadthing` - File upload handling
- **Open Graph**: `/api/og` - Dynamic social media previews

### Component Relationship Diagrams

```mermaid
graph LR
    A[Landing Screen] --> B[Subject Cards]
    A --> C[Navigation Buttons]
    
    B --> D[Mathematics]
    B --> E[Physics]
    B --> F[Chemistry]
    B --> G[Life Sciences]
    
    D --> H[Quiz System]
    E --> I[Physics Quiz]
    F --> J[Practice Quiz]
    G --> K[Interactive Quiz]
    
    H --> L[Question Bank]
    I --> L
    J --> L
    K --> L
    
    L --> M[Progress Tracking]
    M --> N[Performance Analytics]
```

## Key Features

### Functionality Overview

#### Interactive Learning Experience
- **Subject Navigation**: Intuitive subject selection with visual icons and progress indicators
- **Quiz System**: Adaptive quizzing with immediate feedback and detailed explanations
- **Past Paper Access**: Comprehensive archive of previous examination papers
- **Study Planning**: AI-generated personalized study schedules based on student needs

#### AI-Powered Assistance
- **Concept Explanations**: Step-by-step breakdowns of complex topics using South African curriculum standards
- **Study Guidance**: Intelligent recommendations for optimal study strategies
- **Smart Search**: Contextual topic suggestions based on student queries

#### Progress Monitoring
- **Weekly Journeys**: Visual progress tracking with streak indicators
- **Achievement System**: Recognition for completed milestones and challenges
- **Performance Analytics**: Detailed insights into strengths and areas for improvement

### Implementation Highlights

#### Authentication Architecture
- **Social Login**: Support for Google and optional Twitter authentication
- **Anonymous Sessions**: Anonymous user capability for non-authenticated interactions
- **Session Persistence**: Database-backed session storage with configurable expiration

#### Database Design
- **Schema Management**: Drizzle ORM with TypeScript-first schema definitions
- **Migration System**: Automated schema migrations and seed data management
- **Connection Pooling**: Optimized database connections with retry logic

#### Performance Optimization
- **Image Optimization**: Next.js image optimization with remote pattern configuration
- **Code Splitting**: Automatic route-based code splitting for faster initial loads
- **Caching Strategy**: Strategic caching for static content and API responses

### Feature Architecture Diagrams

```mermaid
stateDiagram-v2
    [*] --> Landing
    Landing --> Dashboard: User Authentication
    Landing --> Auth: Guest Access
    
    Auth --> Dashboard: Successful Login
    Auth --> Landing: Cancelled
    
    Dashboard --> SubjectSelection: Browse Content
    SubjectSelection --> QuizSystem: Select Topic
    SubjectSelection --> PastPapers: Access Materials
    
    QuizSystem --> Explanation: Request Help
    QuizSystem --> ProgressTracking: Complete Attempt
    
    Explanation --> QuizSystem: Continue Learning
    ProgressTracking --> SubjectSelection: Review Topics
    
    Dashboard --> Profile: Manage Account
    Profile --> Dashboard: Back to Learning
    
    SubjectSelection --> [*]: Exit Application
```

The MatricMaster AI platform represents a comprehensive educational solution that successfully combines modern web technologies with AI-powered learning assistance. Its modular architecture, robust authentication system, and focus on South African curriculum alignment positions it as a valuable resource for Grade 12 students preparing for their matriculation examinations.

**Sources:**
- [package.json](package.json)
- [next.config.mjs](next.config.mjs)
- [layout.tsx](src/app/layout.tsx)
- [auth.ts](src/lib/auth.ts)
- [db/index.ts](src/lib/db/index.ts)
- [geminiService.ts](src/services/geminiService.ts)
- [aiActions.ts](src/services/aiActions.ts)
- [Landing.tsx](src/screens/Landing.tsx)
- [mock-data.ts](src/constants/mock-data.ts)
- [page.tsx](src/app/page.tsx)
- [button.tsx](src/components/ui/button.tsx)
- [components.json](components.json)

</blog>
